{{Quote|Hah!|Sarrissa Jeng cuts down a Geonosian|audio=Jeng-Hah.flac|<ref name="AOTC" />}}
'''Sarrissa Jeng''' was a [[human]] member of the [[Jedi Order]] during the [[Separatist Crisis]]. In [[22 BBY]], Jeng was one of the 212 [[Jedi]] who traveled to the [[planet]] [[Geonosis]] to rescue the [[Jedi Knight]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]]. During the [[First Battle of Geonosis|battle]], Jeng cut down a [[Geonosian]] with a [[lightsaber]] before the [[Galactic Republic]] and Jedi ultimately emerged victorious over the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|I will take what Jedi we have left and go to Geonosis and help Obi-Wan.|Mace Windu|audio=Windu-WhatJedi.flac|<ref name="AOTC" />}}
[[File:HighAngleArenaBattle-AOTC.png|thumb|right|300px|Sarrissa Jeng fought in the Petranaki Arena.]]
Sarrissa Jeng<ref name="Wanted">{{Topps|set=2015 Topps Star Wars Chrome Perspectives|subset=Jedi Hunt|cardname=Shaak Ti|link=https://www.tcdb.com/ViewCard.cfm/sid/117713/cid/8023100/2015-Topps-Chrome-Star-Wars-Perspectives-Jedi-vs.-Sith---Jedi-Hunt-7-Shaak-TI}}</ref> was a [[human]] who served as a member of the [[Jedi Order]], guardians of the [[Galactic Republic]], during the [[Separatist Crisis]].<ref name="AOTC" /> In [[22 BBY]],<ref name="Atlas">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Atlas: Updated Edition]]'' dates the events of {{Film|II}} to [[22 BBY]].</ref> Jeng was one of<ref name="AOTC" /> 212 [[Jedi]]<ref name="CVD">''[[Star Wars: The Complete Visual Dictionary, New Edition]]''</ref> that made up an [[Jedi assault team|assault team]] led by [[Jedi Master]] [[Mace Windu]] to the [[planet]] [[Geonosis]] in order to rescue the [[Jedi Knight]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]], who had been captured while spying on the [[Executive Separatist Council|leaders]] of the newly formed [[Confederacy of Independent Systems]].<ref name="AOTC" />

Kenobi, along with his [[Padawan]], [[Anakin Skywalker]], and [[Senator]] [[Padmé Amidala]], had been sentenced to [[death]] and faced [[Death penalty|execution]] in<ref name="AOTC" /> the [[Petranaki Arena]].<ref name="CVD" /> While the trio of [[prisoner]]s freed themselves and fought off the beasts that were meant to kill them, Jeng and the other Jedi infiltrated the arena and revealed their presence as Windu confronted [[Count]] [[Dooku]], head of the Confederacy. An [[Separatist Droid Army|army]] of [[battle droid]]s then entered the arena and [[First Battle of Geonosis|attacked]] the Jedi with the support of the [[Homeworld|native]] [[Geonosian]]s, who had joined the Confederacy. While fighting on the arena floor, Jeng gave a battle cry while using a [[lightsaber]] to cut down a [[Warrior caste (Geonosian)|Geonosian warrior]].<ref name="AOTC" />

[[File:ShaakTiGOH.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Sarrissa Jeng was an associate of Shaak Ti (pictured).]]
The Jedi assault team was eventually overwhelmed by the [[droid]]s, who surrounded a group of surviving Jedi, although Jeng was not among them. [[Grand Master]] [[Yoda]] then arrived with the new [[Grand Army of the Republic]] and rescued the survivors before ultimately claiming victory over the Separatists.<ref name="AOTC" /> Of the two hundred and twelve Jedi sent into the arena, only twenty-nine returned.<ref name="Inquisitor">''[[Inquisitor: Rise of the Red Blade]]''</ref> Jeng was at one stage listed as a known associate of [[Jedi Master]] [[Shaak Ti]] on a Separatist wanted poster for the master.<ref name="Wanted" />

==Personality and traits==
Jeng had [[Color|brown]] [[eye]]s, light skin, and brown [[hair]], the latter of which was worn [[braid]]ed in a crown style.<ref name="AOTC" />

==Equipment==
In the arena, Jeng wore [[Jedi apparel|Jedi robes]] that included a dark brown [[Clothing|tunic]] over a light brown shirt. Jeng used a blue-bladed lightsaber in [[Lightsaber combat|combat]].<ref name="AOTC" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:SarrissaJeng-Dead-SkywalkerSaga.png|thumb|right|200px|Sarrissa Jeng's corpse is shown in unused footage from ''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones''.]]
Sarrissa Jeng was created for the [[prequel trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']]<ref name="AOTC" /> which was released on [[May 16]], [[2002]].<ref name="Years">''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, New Edition]]''</ref> The character was portrayed uncredited<ref name="AOTC" /> by [[Karen Wilson]] according to the fan website [http://www.aveleyman.com Aveleyman].<ref name="Aveleyman">{{WebCite|url=https://www.aveleyman.com/ActorCredit.aspx?ActorID=76979|text=Aveleyman - Karen Wilson|work=[http://www.aveleyman.com Aveleyman.com]|archivedate=20210704192627}}</ref> Jeng's body is shown among the dead in an unfinished cut of the film's Battle of Geonosis in which a group of Jedi raid a [[Lucrehulk-class battleship|Droid Control Ship]] in an attempt to shut down the Separatist battle droids.<ref name="Complete Saga">{{HomeVideoCite|set=Star Wars: The Skywalker Saga|scene=Raid on the Droid Control Ship}}</ref> The Jedi battle in the Petranaki Arena scenes were filmed on Stage 3 at [[Fox Studios, Sydney]], between [[August 10]] and [[August 15]], [[2000]].<ref name="Archives">''[[The Star Wars Archives: Episodes I–III, 1999–2005]]''</ref>

In the current ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[canon]], Sarrissa Jeng was first identified on a card in [[2015 Topps Star Wars Chrome Perspectives|2015 Topps ''Star Wars'' Chrome Perspectives: Jedi vs. Sith]], a set released by [[Topps|The Topps Company, Inc.]] in [[2015]].<ref name="Wanted" /> The character's name was then misspelt as "Sarissa Jeng" on a card released via the [[Star Wars: Card Trader|''Star Wars'': Card Trader]] app in [[2021]].<ref name="CT">{{SWCT|set=Fractured - Star Wars: Attack of the Clones|cardname=Sarissa Jeng}}</ref> The [[Sarrissa Jeng/Legends|character]]'s name originated in the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity, appearing prior to the release of ''Attack of the Clones'' in [[HoloNet News Vol. 531 52|''HoloNet News Vol. 531 #52'']], an<ref name="HNN">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|52|regional/13418_4.html|Gotal Guerillas Hijack Commuter Hopper, Take Hostages}}</ref> [[April 18]], 2002<ref name="HNN52 date">{{JCFCite|url=posts/6493893|board=Literature|thread=The Official HoloNetNews Discussion Thread|user=IAmTheDarkSide|date=2002-04-18|time=6:27 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200305125429/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-official-holonetnews-discussion-thread.5990764/page-13}}</ref> issue published on the [[HoloNet News (original website)|HoloNet News]] website as part of the marketing for the film.<ref name="HNN" /> The article was written by [[Pablo Hidalgo]] and [[Paul Ens]].<ref name="Credits">{{HoloNetNewsWeb||credits.html|Credits}}</ref>
